2604.06834.title: On the Step Length Confounding in LLM Reasoning Data Selection
2604.06834.paper_summary: Large reasoning models have recently demonstrated strong performance on complex tasks that require long chain-of-thought reasoning, through supervised fine-tuning on large-scale and high-quality datasets. To construct such datasets, existing pipelines generate long reasoning data from more capable Large Language Models (LLMs) and apply manually heuristic or naturalness-based selection methods to filter high-quality samples. Despite the proven effectiveness of naturalness-based data selection, which ranks data by the average log probability assigned by LLMs, our analysis shows that, when applied to LLM reasoning datasets, it systematically prefers samples with longer reasoning steps (i.e., more tokens per step) rather than higher-quality ones, a phenomenon we term step length confounding. Through quantitative analysis, we attribute this phenomenon to low-probability first tokens in reasoning steps; longer steps dilute their influence, thereby inflating the average log probabilities. To address this issue, we propose two variant methods: ASLEC-DROP, which drops first-token probabilities when computing average log probability, and ASLEC-CASL, which applies a causal debiasing regression to remove the first tokens' confounding effect. Experiments across four LLMs and five evaluation benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in mitigating the step length confounding problem.
2604.06834.paper_summary_zh: 大型推理模型最近在需要長鏈思考推理的複雜任務上展現出強大的表現，這是通過在大規模和高質量數據集上進行監督性微調實現的。為了構建這樣的數據集，現有的流程從更強大的大型語言模型（LLMs）生成長推理數據，並應用手動啟發式或自然性基礎的選擇方法來篩選高質量樣本。儘管基於自然性的數據選擇已被證明有效，該方法是通過LLMs分配的平均對數概率來對數據進行排名，但我們的分析顯示，當應用於LLM推理數據集時，它系統性地偏好具有較長推理步驟的樣本（即，每步的標記數更多），而非質量更高的樣本，這一現象我們稱之為步長混淆。通過定量分析，我們將這一現象歸因於推理步驟中的低概率首標記；較長的步驟稀釋了它們的影響，從而抬高了平均對數概率。為了解決這一問題，我們提出了兩種變體方法：ASLEC-DROP，在計算平均對數概率時丟棄首標記的概率，以及ASLEC-CASL，應用因果去偏回歸來消除首標記的混淆效應。在四個LLM和五個評估基準上的實驗證明了我們的方法在減輕步長混淆問題上的有效性。
2604.06834.author: Bing Wang et.al.
2604.06834.authors: Bing Wang, Rui Miao, Chen Shen, Shaotian Yan, Kaiyuan Liu, Ximing Li, Xiaosong Yuan, Sinan Fan, Jun Zhang, Jieping Ye
